input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's your complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS:

---

## 1. Install Xcode (iOS builds)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- Install Xcode Command Line Tools: `xcode-select --install`
- Open Xcode once to accept the license and install components
- **Version discovery:** MAUI release notes specify the minimum Xcode version. Check [github.com/dotnet/maui/wiki](https://github.com/dotnet/maui/wiki) or the workload manifest.

## 2. Install .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) — get the latest LTS or STS release that supports MAUI
- Use the `.pkg` installer for macOS (arm64 for Apple Silicon, x64 for Intel)
- **Verify:** `dotnet --version`

## 3. Install the MAUI Workload

```bash
sudo dotnet workload install maui
```

- **Verify:** `dotnet workload list` — should show `maui` as installed
- **Discover available versions:** `dotnet workload search maui`

## 4. Install JDK (Android builds)

- The MAUI workload installs a compatible JDK via the `android` workload. Check with:
  ```bash
  dotnet workload list
  ```
  If `android` is listed, the JDK/SDK are managed for you under `~/Library/Developer/Xamarin/`.
- **If you need to install manually:** [Microsoft OpenJDK](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/java/openjdk/download) (JDK 17 is currently required)
- **Verify:** `java -version`

## 5. Android SDK & Emulator

- The `android` workload auto-provisions the Android SDK. If you need more control:
  - Install [Android Studio](https://developer.android.com/studio) → SDK Manager for additional API levels/images
  - Or use the command-line `sdkmanager`
- **Verify:** `dotnet build -t:InstallAndroidDependencies -f net9.0-android` (accepts licenses & installs missing pieces)

## 6. Create & Run a Test Project

```bash
dotnet new maui -n TestApp
cd TestApp

# iOS (requires a simulator or device)
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios
# Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android
```

## 7. Full Verification Checklist

| Check | Command |
|---|---|
| .NET SDK | `dotnet --info` |
| MAUI workload | `dotnet workload list` |
| Xcode CLI tools | `xcode-select -p` |
| Xcode version | `xcodebuild -version` |
| JDK | `java -version` |
| iOS Simulator | `xcrun simctl list devices available` |
| Android emulator | `emulator -list-avds` |
| Build iOS | `dotnet build -f net9.0-ios` |
| Build Android | `dotnet build -f net9.0-android` |

## 8. Optional but Recommended

- **IDE:** [Visual Studio Code](https://code.visualstudio.com/) + the [.NET MAUI extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-dotnettools.dotnet-maui), or [JetBrains Rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/)
- **Hot Reload:** built into `dotnet` CLI and the VS Code extension

## Key Version-Discovery Tip

Run `dotnet workload update` periodically — it pulls the latest compatible Android SDK, build tools, and MAUI templates matched to your .NET SDK version, so you don't have to track version matrices manually.
